$comment: SOURCE OF TRUTH for the Endpoint Contracts rules. Ratified by Jon 2026-06-12 ('let's get the contract implemented') executing the sim-style endpoint contract plan (Planned: kai-chattr-sim-style-endpoint-contract-implementation-plan-draft.md, rev 3).
title: Endpoint Contracts
summary: Contract-first endpoint registry: every safe HTTP route owns exactly one explicit EndpointContract; canonical URL rules and observability attribute rules are test-enforced.

rules.text: Every safe-method FastAPI route (DELETE/GET/PATCH/POST/PUT, excluding OpenAPI docs) must own exactly one EndpointContract in services/api/app/endpoint_contract_registry.py keyed by (method, path_template), declaring auth, proxy, surface, scope, canonical_status, data_owner, and request/response model names. No orphan contracts.
services/api/tests/fixtures/endpoint_route_inventory.txt is the canonical route inventory. Adding or removing a route without updating the fixture (and its contract) fails tests in both directions.
No route path may contain a user identifier (user_id or equivalent). Authentication determines the user; URLs carry only opaque tenant and session identifiers.
Any contract with scope workspace_session must declare tenant_param and session_param, and both must appear as template tokens in the path.

canonical_status is one of: canonical (conforms to target product canon), legacy (live pre-tenancy surface awaiting rescope), internal (infra/agent-wrapper lane), redirect_helper (alias permitted by canon rule 7). New product routes must be canonical; new legacy entries require explicit ratification.

Endpoint span/metric attributes carry registry metadata only (kai_chattr.endpoint.*, http.request.method, http.route). user_id, session_hash, email, tokens, raw filenames, request payloads, and message text are forbidden in trace and metric attributes.

GET /observability/endpoints and endpoint span attributes read from the endpoint contract registry, not from path-prefix heuristics. GET /schemas/endpoint-contracts/status reports coverage and must show zero uncontracted routes and zero orphans.

enforcement: pytest: tests/test_endpoint_contract_registry.py (coverage, drift-lock, URL canon gates)
pytest: tests/test_observability_contract.py (registry-backed catalog, forbidden span attributes)
openItems: Frontend raw-call boundary test lands with plan Task 7 and extends enforcedBy.
Legacy-scoped routes are rescoped to workspace/session canon by the route-promotion program; each promotion flips canonical_status.
